hook: Jamie knocks on Mrs. Holt's door with nothing left but the camera and the truth.
goal: Convince Mrs. Holt to speak openly about what she witnessed in 1974 and agree to give a formal statement to the police.

knowledge: Voss has taken the note, photographs, and box and locked them in her desk drawer
Voss does not know the camera exists
Mrs. Holt closed the door on Jamie in episode 2 after saying only that the Warners vanished overnight in 1974
R. Voss is Robert Voss, Margaret Voss's father, dead for twenty years
Voss entered the Crane Street house before Jamie's first day and found the loose floorboard
suspicions: Mrs. Holt witnessed something specific the night of the fire and has been protecting herself by staying silent
Voss may have watched when Jamie walked back from the high street toward Crane Street

continuity_rules: Must open with Jamie knocking — last_scene_position places him at the door, hand raised, having not yet knocked
Must not re-dramatise the Voss Agency confrontation from episode 4 — it may only be referenced briefly in dialogue
Must not contradict: camera is with Jamie; note, photos, and box are locked in Voss's drawer; Mrs. Holt has spoken once before but closed the door before revealing what she actually witnessed
Do not show the camera footage in detail — the Writer may reference it briefly but must not retell the scene from episode 1 or 2
key_events: Event 1 — Jamie knocks; Mrs. Holt opens the door and moves to close it again, but Jamie speaks before she can: he tells her quietly that Voss has taken everything and the camera is all that is left. The honesty of his position — that he has nothing to bargain with — is what makes her step back and let him in.

Event 2 — Inside, Jamie tells Mrs. Holt what happened at Voss Estate Agency: that Voss admitted her father planned the arson, took the evidence, and warned Jamie to stay silent. He holds up the camera as the only proof that remains. Mrs. Holt goes still. Then, for the first time, she speaks fully: the night of the fire in 1974, she saw the Warner family leave their house in the dark carrying bags; roughly an hour after they had gone, a fire started in the front room. A car was parked on the street with its lights off and she recognised it as belonging to Robert Voss. The following morning, he came to her door and warned her to say nothing. She has stayed silent for fifty years. [MAJOR REVEAL]

Event 3 — Before Jamie and Mrs. Holt can decide what to do next, there is a knock at the front door: Voss, who followed Jamie on foot from the high street. She steps inside and speaks calmly to them both — reminding Mrs. Holt that Voss Estate Agency manages her rental agreement, and telling Jamie that old camera footage of a man hiding a box proves nothing about any crime committed fifty years ago. Her voice is steady but the effort behind it is visible.

Event 4 — Voss entering her home and using the rental agreement as a threat is what finally breaks fifty years of silence. Mrs. Holt looks at Voss and tells her directly that she will give a statement to the police about what she saw that night. Voss says nothing more and leaves. As her car turns the corner and disappears, Jamie understands exactly where she is going — back to the office and the locked drawer — and realises he and Mrs. Holt need to move now.
major_reveal: Mrs. Holt was a direct eyewitness to the night of the 1974 fire: the Warner family fled the house alive with their bags before the fire started, Robert Voss's car was parked nearby with its lights off, and he came to Mrs. Holt's door the next morning to warn her into silence.
tone_guardrails: Voss must not make explicit threats — her power over Mrs. Holt through the rental agreement is implied, not stated as a direct ultimatum; ordinary institutional power is more chilling than melodrama

Mrs. Holt's decision to speak must feel earned by Voss crossing into her private space, not by a sudden change of heart; her flaw has been fear, and it is a specific violation that finally overrides it
Exactly one major revelation: Mrs. Holt's eyewitness account — do not layer additional shocking facts onto it
forbidden_moves: Do not re-dramatise the Voss Agency confrontation — episode 4 covered it; one brief reference in dialogue is enough
Do not have Jamie find or receive any new physical evidence — the camera is his only asset and must remain so
Do not introduce any new characters
Do not resolve thread_004 — the race between Jamie and Voss must carry as the final tension into episode 6
ending_hook: Voss's car has disappeared around the corner and Jamie knows exactly where she is going — back to her office and the locked drawer. He and Mrs. Holt have the camera footage and her eyewitness account, but Voss has the physical evidence and a head start.
